dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I'm looking for someone. I've been looking for a very long time.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My sister left {location} three seasons ago to trade {quest_item} with the settlements up north. She never arrived. Everyone says the {enemy_type} got her on the road, but no body was ever found. No blood, no wreckage, nothing. Last week, a merchant passing through mentioned seeing a woman matching her description living in a camp {location_hint}. Alone, but alive. I need someone to go there and find out if it's really her. I can't make the journey myself: my legs haven't been right since the winter.
dialogueChunks.outro: If she's alive, she needs to know we never stopped looking. That we never gave up on her.

playerResponses.text: I'll find her. If she's out there, I'll bring word.
Three seasons is a long time. Are you sure you're ready for what I might find?
Missing for that long... I don't want to be the one bringing bad news.

dialogueChunks.intro: I know what people think, {player_name}. They think I'm chasing ghosts. But I know what I saw.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} disappeared during a {enemy_type} raid on {location} six months ago. The guards assumed the worst and held a memorial. But two weeks ago, I was gathering {quest_item} {location_hint} and I found fresh boot prints: their boots, {player_name}. I know the repair pattern on the sole because I stitched it myself. Someone is out there, moving through the wilderness, and I believe it's {target_npc}. They might be injured, lost, or hiding. Either way, they need help.
dialogueChunks.outro: Everyone else has accepted their death. I haven't. And until I see a grave with my own eyes, I won't.

playerResponses.text: Boot prints are evidence. I'll follow the trail.
Hope is stubborn. I'll find out if it's justified.
Six months in the wild alone? I don't want to give you false hope.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need to show you something. Come inside where nobody can hear us.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: This letter arrived at {location} yesterday, left at the gate overnight. No messenger, no name on the outside. But I recognize the handwriting: it's from {target_npc}, my apprentice who vanished two years ago during an expedition {location_hint}. The letter says they're alive but can't return: something about being held by a group that won't let them leave. They need {quest_item} to trade for their freedom, and they're begging for someone to bring it to a drop point near the old ruins. The {enemy_type} between here and there make a direct journey dangerous.
dialogueChunks.outro: Two years I thought they were dead. Two years of guilt for sending them on that expedition. Now there's a chance to bring them home.

playerResponses.text: A letter after two years. This changes everything. I'll go.
It could be a trap using their handwriting. But I'll check it out.
A mysterious letter and dangerous territory. This screams ambush.
